ddls:
CREATE TABLE "posts" (
  "post_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each post */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the content post */,
  "slug" TEXT /* SEO-friendly URL slug for the post */,
  "body" TEXT /* Content of the post in text or markdown format */,
  "publish_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the post was published */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the post was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the post was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the post (e.g., draft, published) */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the author who wrote the post */,
  "content_type" TEXT /* Type of content (e.g., article, blog post, scientific publication) */,
  "featured_image" TEXT /* URL of the featured image */,
  "categories" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of categories */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of tags */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biography of the author */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL of the author's profile picture */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "post_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update record */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post being updated */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the update occurred */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Description of the changes made */,
  "update_type" TEXT /* Type of update (e.g., minor, major) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_post_updates_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post being analyzed */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views the post has received */,
  "shares" INTEGER /* Number of times the post has been shared */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the post has received */,
  "comments" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the post */,
  "click_through_rate" REAL /* Click-through rate (CTR) of the post */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user's account */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, editor, author) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role that has the permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission (e.g., create_post, edit_post, publish_post) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "post_categories" (
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id", "category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "post_tags" (
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post */,
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id", "tag_id")
)
column_names: SEO-friendly URL slug for the post
